title: rephrase with keeping references: Despite the increasing importance of digital transformation in healthcare systems, the…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: rephrase with keeping references: Despite the increasing importance of digital transformation in healthcare systems, the path towards the digital transformation of hospitals still presents some problems(Raimo et al., 2023). As a result, most digital transformation projects have not lived up to expectations (Preko and Boateng, 2020). A recent systematic literature review conducted by Marques and Ferreira (2020) highlighted a sharp increase in the last twenty years of studies on the topic of digital transformation in healthcare originations such as hospitals. However, the authors highlighted that these studies focused mainly on specific issues such as integrated management, medical images, electronic medical records and virtual services. The contributions that dealt with the digital transformation of hospitals from a broader perspective focused mainly on the identification of the different digital technologies implemented, on the advantages and benefits deriving from the implementation of digital technologies and on the barriers and challenges associated with the implementation of these technologies (Tortorella et al., 2020a). However, there is an important gap in the academic literature since only a limited number of studies have examined the drivers capable of influencing the level of digital transformation of hospitals, so empirical research on the drivers of digital transformation of hospitals is still lacking (Raimo et al., 2023; Kraus et al., 2021b).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Despite the increasing significance of digital transformation in healthcare systems, there remain some challenges in the path towards the digital transformation of hospitals (Raimo et al., 2023). Thus, most digital transformation projects have not met their expectations (Preko and Boateng, 2020). According to a recent systematic literature review conducted by Marques and Ferreira (2020), there has been a substantial growth in studies on the subject of digital transformation in healthcare organizations, such as hospitals, over the past two decades. However, these studies mainly focused on particular issues, including integrated management, medical imaging, electronic medical records, and virtual services. The contributions that looked into the digital transformation of hospitals from a broader perspective mainly concentrated on recognizing the different digital technologies implemented, the advantages and benefits resulting from implementing digital technologies, and the barriers and challenges related to their implementation (Tortorella et al., 2020a). Nevertheless, there is a crucial gap in the existing literature, with only a few studies investigating the drivers capable of influencing the level of digital transformation of hospitals. Hence, empirical research on the drivers of digital transformation of hospitals is still inadequate (Raimo et al., 2023; Kraus et al., 2021b).

However, the authors highlighted that these studies focused mainly on specific issues such as integrated management, medical images, electronic medical records and virtual services. The contributions that dealt with the digital transformation of hospitals from a broader perspective focused mainly on the identification of the different digital technologies implemented, on the advantages and benefits deriving from the implementation of digital technologies and on the barriers and challenges associated with the implementation of these technologies (Tortorella et al., 2020a). turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Marques and Ferreira (2020) conducted a systematic literature review, which revealed a notable increase in the number of studies conducted over the last two decades on the subject of digital transformation in healthcare organizations, such as hospitals. However, the authors pointed out that the focus of these studies was centered mainly on specific issues like integrated management, medical imaging, electronic medical records, and virtual services. Contributions that aimed to provide a comprehensive viewpoint on transforming hospitals digitally focused mainly on identifying various digital technologies implemented, elaborating the advantages and benefits derived from their application, and outlining the associated barriers and challenges (Tortorella et al., 2020a). However, there remains a significant gap in academic literature since only a few studies have investigated the drivers capable of influencing the degree of digital transformation in hospitals. Therefore, there is a need for empirical research on the drivers of digital transformation initiatives in hospitals (Raimo et al., 2023; Kraus et al., 2021b).
